name: ebay_advanced
title: eBay Advanced Search Automation
description: Complete a real multi-input search form with keywords, price range, checkboxes, and submit.
prompt: Go to eBay advanced search, fill multiple fields, set filters, and run the search.

steps.caption: Step 1: Open eBay Advanced Search
steps.narration: Let's start by opening eBay Advanced Search. It gives the walkthrough a clean starting point before the real automation work begins.

steps.caption: Step 2: Enter main search keywords
steps.narration: Next, we enter main search keywords. A little data entry here saves the same keystrokes on every future run.

steps.caption: Step 3: Change the keyword match dropdown to exact order
steps.description: Change the keyword match dropdown to exact order
steps.narration: From there, we change the keyword match dropdown to exact order. It keeps the flow easy to follow without sounding like a checklist.

steps.caption: Step 4: Enter words to exclude from results
steps.description: Enter words to exclude from results
steps.narration: Now we enter words to exclude from results. A little data entry here saves the same keystrokes on every future run.

steps.caption: Step 5: Include title and description in the search scope
steps.description: Include title and description in the search scope
steps.narration: After that, we include title and description in the search scope. It keeps the flow easy to follow without sounding like a checklist.

steps.caption: Step 6: Set minimum and maximum price filters
steps.description: Set minimum and maximum price filters
steps.narration: Next, we set minimum and maximum price filters. Being explicit here keeps reruns consistent.

steps.caption: Step 7: Filter for new-condition items
steps.narration: From there, we filter for new-condition items. It keeps the flow easy to follow without sounding like a checklist.

steps.caption: Step 8: Run the search and open the filtered results page
steps.description: Run the search and open the filtered results page
steps.narration: Now we run the search and open the filtered results page. Once we run it, the page does the heavy lifting for us.
